cod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cocama.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
coitus: noun. Sexual intercourse, especially that involving penile – vaginal penetration.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
conduit: noun. A channel or pathway through which something is conducted, carried, etc.
cost: verb. To incur a charge of; to require payment of a (specified) price.
cud: noun. The portion of food which is brought back into the mouth by ruminants from their rumen, to be chewed a second time.
cunt: noun. The female genitalia, especially the vulva.
cut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teutila Cuicatec.
cutin: noun. A waxy polymer of hydroxy acids that is the main constituent of plant cuticle.
cutis: noun. The true skin or dermis, underlying the epidermis.
dicot: noun. A plant whose seedlings have two cotyledons, a dicotyledon.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dimasa.
disc: noun. A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.
disco: noun. Clipping of discotheque (“nightclub for dancing”).
discount: verb. To sell at a reduced price.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
doc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern Kam.
doit: noun. A small Dutch coin, equivalent to one-eighth of a stiver.
don: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Doura.
duct: noun. A pipe, tube or canal which carries gas or liquid from one place to another.
dui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dumun.
duit: noun. Alternative form of doit (“a small Dutch coin”)
duits: noun. Alternative form of doits; plural of duit
dun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dusun Deyah.
dunt: noun. A stroke; a dull-sounding blow.
duo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dupaningan Agta.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
ids: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idesa.
incus: noun. A small anvil -shaped bone in the middle ear.
induct: verb. To bring in as a member; to make a part of.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
nous: noun. The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
nudist: noun. A person who practices nudism.
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
scudo: noun. A silver coin and unit of currency of various Italian states from the 16th to the 19th centuries.
scut: noun. A hare; a hare as the game in a hunt.
sic: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Malinguat.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
snot: noun. Mucus, especially mucus from the nose.
snout: noun. The long, projecting nose, mouth, and jaw of a beast, as of pigs.
sod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Songoora.
sodic: adjective. Of, relating to, or containing sodium.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sonic: adjective. Of or relating to sound.
sot: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sotho.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
stoic: noun. Proponent of stoicism, a school of thought, from in 300 B.C.E. up to about the time of Marcus Aurelius, who holds that by cultivating
stud: noun. A male animal, especially a stud horse (stallion), kept for breeding.
studio: noun. An artist’s or photographer’s workshop or the room in which an artist works.
stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tics: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of tic
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
tocsin: noun. An alarm or other signal sounded by a bell or bells, originally especially with reference to France.
tod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Toma.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
tunic: noun. A garment worn over the torso, with or without sleeves, and of various lengths reaching from the hips to the ankles.
undo: verb. To reverse the effects of an action.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
